goals.title: Full C99 compiler in Rust: use clang as oracle for complex C99 and un...
goals.objective: Full C99 compiler in Rust: use clang as oracle for complex C99 and undefined/implementation-defined edge cases, keep Doom public source compiling/linking 62/62, work on main with push-per-commit, preserve .omo/rules strict Rust constraints, and continue oracle-driven bug fixing toward full C99 coverage. Current concrete increment: recover failed review and land function-pointer typedef compound literal array support with focused oracle, manual runtime QA, full oracle, Doom scan, strict quality/slop review, mandatory reviewer approval, commit and push.

successCriteria.scenario: Function-pointer typedef compound literal arrays match clang and run manually
successCriteria.expectedEvidence: CLI stdout from focused oracle shows deep_c99_function_pointer_typedef_compound: 3 passed; 0 failed; manual temp C compiled by c99inrust prints manual-fnptr-typedef-compound and exits 0.
successCriteria.capturedEvidence: QA-A focused oracle post-refactor: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/c99inrust-target-fnptr CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 cargo test --test clang_oracle deep_c99_function_pointer_typedef_compound -- --nocapture --test-threads=1 -> test result ok, 3 passed, 0 failed, 628 filtered out. QA-B manual runtime post-refactor: temp source /var/folders/nj/hqfr8ndn5q56cqw7jqgbrck40000gn/T/c99inrust-fnptr-manual-postrefactor.XXXXXX.lMBMSehjkv/fnptr_typedef_compound.c compiled with /tmp/c99inrust-target-fnptr/debug/c99inrust; executable stdout manual-fnptr-typedef-compound; exit 0.

successCriteria.scenario: Strict quality, dirty-worktree, and slop/refactor safety for changed Rust files
successCriteria.expectedEvidence: CLI/LSP stdout proves fmt, no-excuses, git diff --check, clippy -D warnings, pure LOC <=250, and LSP diagnostics pass; slop/refactor report lists no required behavior-preserving cleanup or records safe cleanup plus rerun evidence; git status proves sionickimi remains untracked and unstaged.
successCriteria.capturedEvidence: QA-E strict quality/slop after cleanup: cargo fmt --all -- --check exited 0; tools/check-rust-no-excuses.sh on changed Rust/test files exited 0; git diff --check exited 0; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/c99inrust-target-fnptr CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings exited 0; LSP diagnostics found no diagnostics on representative changed files; pure LOC all <=250 with parser_statement_dispatch.rs reduced to 136 and new parser_declaration_statement.rs 114; slop/refactor report records only safe single-responsibility split and no remaining required cleanup; git state preserved excluded sionickimi as untracked.

successCriteria.scenario: Full clang oracle, Doom 62/62 regression, mandatory review, commit and push
successCriteria.expectedEvidence: CLI stdout proves full cargo test --test clang_oracle passes with zero failed; Doom scan stdout shows compile_ok=62 compile_fail=0 and link_status=0; codex-ultrawork-reviewer returns unconditional approval; git commit SHA/subject and git push stdout prove main pushed; rev-parse shows HEAD == origin/main.
successCriteria.capturedEvidence: QA-C full oracle PASS: cargo test --test clang_oracle -> 631 passed, 0 failed. QA-D Doom scan PASS: compile_ok=62 compile_fail=0, link_status=0, binary=/tmp/c99inrust-doom-link-scan-fnptr-typedef-compound-postrefactor/linuxdoom-c99inrust. Review PASS: /root/review_fnptr_typedef_compound_final returned UNCONDITIONAL APPROVAL. Delivery PASS: commit 9e9c5c807b8ed019066af908184eecccfb5b9006 fix(c99): support function pointer typedef compound arrays pushed to origin/main; git rev-parse HEAD origin/main both 9e9c5c807b8ed019066af908184eecccfb5b9006.

codexObjective: Complete the durable ultragoal plan in .omo/ultragoal/goals.json, including later accepted/appended stories, under the original brief constraints; use .omo/ultragoal/ledger.jsonl as the audit trail.
